# Flywheel Analysis — March 2, 2026

*Generated by NASR | Weekly Data Flywheel Cron | Run time: 2026-03-02 23:59 UTC*

---

## Response Rate

- Applications sent: 45
- Responses received: 1 confirmed (Delphi Consulting, interview stage)
- Response rate: 1/45 = 2.2%
- Average response time: 3 days (Delphi applied Feb 21, interview Feb 24)
- Gmail scan: UNAVAILABLE — ahmednasr999@gmail.com returned 403 insufficientPermissions. Data limited to pipeline.md only.

---

## What Is Working

- **Sectors generating responses:** AI/PM consulting (Delphi Consulting — Senior AI PM, interview confirmed)
- **Job boards generating responses:** Direct recruiter outreach via Delphi (not LinkedIn self-apply)
- **CV ATS score threshold:** The one confirmed callback (Delphi) had a 91% ATS score. All other 91%+ CVs (Al-Gihaz COO, Confidential Head AI, Apex AI, Halian PMO, Windmills CTO, NXS Director AI, Auxo VP AI, UnlockLand VP Ops, Cooper Fitch, SmartChoice, Faithful Executive, Salt) have not yet generated visible pipeline responses. 91% appears to be the minimum viable threshold for competitive applications, but is not sufficient alone.
- **Application volume surge:** 33 of 45 applications were submitted on Feb 27 alone, indicating a strong single-day batch effort.
- **LinkedIn content:** Performance data is NOT tracked yet. The LinkedIn content calendar exists and Week 1 posts (Feb 25-26) were marked for posting, but the Performance Tracking table in linkedin_content_calendar.md shows zero engagement data recorded for any week. No pattern can be drawn from data that does not exist.

---

## What Is Not Working

- **Sectors with zero confirmed responses:** FinTech (FAB VP Technology), HealthTech (Proximie, Dubai Health, Sky News Arabia), Consulting (Buro Happold, Oracle, Renard, Cooper Fitch, eMagine, One Executive, Cygnify), Government/Quasi-Gov (Dubai Holding x2, King Salman Airport, Halian Federal PMO, Confidential Group IT Director Bahrain), AI/Tech (AIQU, NXS, Auxo, Apex, Codesearch, Hays Head AI, SmartChoice, Confidential Head AI)
- **Job boards with zero confirmed responses:** LinkedIn (all 35+ LinkedIn applications show no confirmed response in pipeline). Direct portals (Buro Happold careers portal, Oracle portal, FAB Oracle portal) also show no response.
- **Patterns in silence:** The bulk application batch (33 roles on Feb 27) is only 3-4 days old — most are inside the typical recruiter review cycle (1-3 weeks). It is too early to draw conclusions on silence vs. no-interest for that batch. The outlier is roles applied Feb 21-26 (Delphi, Talabat CPTO, AIQU, Nexus, eMagine, Confidential Bahrain) which now have 4-10 days of elapsed time with only Delphi responding.
- **ATS score outliers with no response yet:** Dubai Health (68-72%) and Oracle (65-70%) are below the apparent 87%+ working threshold. These were stretch applications and silence is expected.
- **Missing Gmail data:** Unable to detect recruiter outreach or rejection emails that may not be reflected in pipeline.md. This is a material data gap.

---

## Flywheel Recommendations

- **Job Radar:** Maintain current filter focus on AI/Digital Transformation/PMO hybrid roles at Director/VP/C-suite level with ATS 87%+. The Delphi win validates the AI PM angle. Prioritize roles where Ahmed has direct prior-company authority-jacking potential (Delivery Hero ecosystem, healthcare IT, fintech).
- **CV Strategy:** 91% ATS appears to be the competitive floor. Roles with CVs below 85% (Dubai Health 68-72%, Oracle 65-70%, Teleperformance 72-78%) generated no signal. Do not apply below 85% going forward unless the role is a high-priority strategic target with a personal connection. For the 87-91% band, continue optimizing toward 92%+ where possible.
- **LinkedIn:** No engagement data exists yet. The content calendar is built and posts started Feb 25. Week 2 posts (Mar 2-6) are the first full week. Priority action: start recording impressions, likes, and comments in the Performance Tracking table weekly so the flywheel has fuel by Week 4 analysis.
- **Application focus:** The Feb 27 batch (33 roles) enters its first follow-up window Mar 6-10. Do not add new volume until that batch shows conversion signal. Concentrate next week on: (1) Delphi follow-up momentum, (2) any recruiter responses from the batch, (3) LinkedIn engagement to generate inbound.

---

## One Actionable Change for This Week

**Fix Gmail access before the next flywheel run.** The biggest gap in this analysis is zero visibility into recruiter emails. If any of the Feb 27 batch contacted Ahmed by email and pipeline.md was not updated, NASR is flying blind. Re-authorize ahmednasr999@gmail.com with full Gmail read scope (run: `gog auth add ahmednasr999@gmail.com --services gmail`). This unlocks: response time tracking, rejection vs. interest classification, recruiter outreach patterns. Without it, the flywheel analysis is running on 20% of its intended data.
